reasoning: The base rate for the US government declaring an astronomical object to be extraterrestrial life or technology is 0. Historically, even highly anomalous objects like 'Oumuamua generated speculation but were never confirmed as aliens, as science and government require overwhelming proof and years of consensus-building before making such historic announcements. Even if this interstellar object (A11pl3Z) displayed highly unusual characteristics around its closest approach in late October 2025, reaching a definitive, official declaration from the US President or a Cabinet-level agency by December 31, 2025, is practically impossible. Therefore, the probability is set to the lowest allowed threshold.
